---
title: "HCI:<br>Analytic Evaluation"
author: Mick McQuaid
date: today
bibliography: master.bib
format:
  revealjs:
    logo: iSchoolLogo.png
    theme: moon
    css: style.css
    transition: slide
    background-transition: fade
    preview-links: auto
    controls: true
    controls-layout: bottom-right
    center: true
---

::: {.r-fit-text}
Week TEN
:::

# Today

# Q and A from last time

All the questions were about Figma!

##

![](fiTitanicFigma.png)

::: {.notes}
I personally think Nudelman is completely wrong but it is a catchy graphic. Nudelman's claim is that chatbox interfaces are the future and Figma isn't equipped to handle them. I think chat interfaces are poor interfaces and that the industry is yet to hit on the best interface for an AI-first application. Figma may be doomed when that day comes. But it will depend, in part, on what that interface is. None of knows right now what it will be.
:::

# Discussion

# Design Critique

Julia

# Article Presentation
Let's look at `Morris2025.pdf`

# Analytic Evaluation

Let's look at [HCI experiments](https://mickmcquaid.com/stats/studyGuide/week13.html)

# Readings

Readings last week include @Hartson2019: Ch 22--24
Readings this week include @Hartson2019: Ch 25--26

# Assignments
Milestone 4

# References

::: {#refs}
:::

---

::: {.r-fit-text}
END
:::

# Colophon

This slideshow was produced using `quarto`

Fonts are *League Gothic* and *Lato*
